candidates.row_label: Amortization of Patents in Cost of Revenues
candidates.why: Appears directly under the selected parent row 'Cost of goods sold' as a COGS sub-component, so it is already embedded in the selected COGS total and should not be added separately.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Expenditure for Development in Cost of Revenues
candidates.why: Listed within the COGS section beneath 'Cost of goods sold'; looks like a component of the selected parent bucket rather than a separate same-level operating line.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Intangibles excluding Goodwill in Cost of Revenues
candidates.why: Presented as a COGS sub-component under the selected 'Cost of goods sold' total, indicating inclusion in the chosen COGS row.

Explicitly labelled as COGS depreciation, but shown as a component within the selected 'Cost of goods sold' bucket together with 'Other Cost of Goods Sold', so adding it separately would double count.

variables.notes: The selected COGS reference bucket is the parent total 'Cost of goods sold'. The excerpt shows detailed COGS components beneath it, including depreciation and amortization lines plus 'Other Cost of Goods Sold', which indicates the D&A is already embedded in the selected COGS row.

candidates.why: Shown directly under the selected parent row 'Selling expenses' as a component of that expense bucket, so it is already included in the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS total.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Appears as a detailed SG&A component beneath selected Selling/Administrative expense lines, not as a separate same-level operating line. Adding it would double count against the selected parent rows.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Patents in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Presented as a granular amortization component within the selected SG&A buckets, so it should not be added separately.
Nested under the selected parent row 'Selling expenses', indicating it is already embedded in that selected XSGA component.
Although explicitly SG&A-related, it is listed as a component under 'Administrative expenses', one of the selected XSGA parent rows, so separate addition would double count.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Detailed amortization line within SG&A, appearing beneath selected parent SG&A rows rather than alongside them as a separate operating line.
Clearly a component of the selected 'Administrative expenses' row and therefore already embedded in the selected XSGA bucket.

This is a component under the selected parent row 'Research and development costs'. Since that R&D row is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, the special R&D rule also argues against adding R&D D&A separately.
Appears as a sub-component of the selected 'Research and development costs' row, and R&D is already explicitly handled in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, so it should not be added separately.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA reference bucket is the combined total of 'Selling expenses', 'Administrative expenses', and 'Research and development costs'. The visible D&A rows are subordinate components within those selected parent rows, not separate same-level lines. Also, the dedicated R&D expense row is already selected for both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, so R&D-related D&A should not be added separately.
